Episode 896: The 5 Secrets of Successful People

February 5, 2025 by Ivan Misner 10 Comments

Podcast: Download

Subscribe: Apple Podcasts | RSS | Subscription Options

Print This Episode

Synopsis

Y.P. Lai, National Director for BNI Thailand and BNI Philippines, explains that the five key fundamentals that BNI asks members to do are actually fundamental business principles that follow universal laws of success.

  1. Turn up to impress.
  2. Continuous learning.
  3. Build connections and relationships.
  4. Create value for your business associates.
  5. Help your clients to grow their business.

If you plug into the five key fundamentals, you’ll be successful in your life and your business through BNI.

Complete Transcript of Episode 896

Priscilla:
Hello everybody, and welcome back to The Official BNI Podcast. I’m Priscilla Rice, and I’m coming to you from Live Oak recording studio in Berkeley, California, and I’m joined on the phone today by the Founder and the Chief Visionary Officer of BNI, Dr. Ivan Misner. Hello, Ivan, how are you and where are you?

Ivan:
I just did a Board Meeting in Charlotte where the BNI Global Headquarters is. And for everyone listening to this, this is International Networking Week®, the first full week of February. So welcome to International Networking Week.

Priscilla:
Great.

Ivan:
And I’m really excited to have somebody from our international team here today. His name is Y.P. Lai. Y.P. is an Amazon Best Selling Author, a master global trainer, a certified dream coach. I’m guessing that’s with Michael Gerber?

Y.P.:
That’s with Marcia, one of our keynote speakers at the BNI Global Convention in Poland.

Ivan:
Yeah, we’ve had some pretty amazing speakers at the Global Conventions. So Y.P. has trained thousands of business owners worldwide. He’s a National Director for BNI Thailand and BNI Philippines. He has over 30 years of corporate experience. I didn’t know this about you, Y.P. You trained as a civil engineer.

Y.P.:
(chuckling) Yes

Ivan:
Y.P. simplifies complex ideas into actionable steps. He is absolutely a mentor to many, many BNI Directors and members throughout the world. Y.P. welcome to BNI Podcast.

Y.P.:
Thank you. Thank you so much, Dr Ivan. And it’s a pleasure to be here and good to, you know, hear you in person, Priscilla. I’ve been hearing so many podcasts.

Priscilla:
Oh, thank you.

Ivan:
Lots and lots of podcasts. And Y.P., we’ve known each other for many, many years. Just call me Ivan, please.

Y.P.:
Okay, yeah, sure, Ivan.

Ivan:
So today’s topic is the Five Secrets of Successful People. So could you share with us the five key fundamentals that members often see as a BNI thing, but in fact, it’s really what successful people do.

Y.P.:
Okay, just before I share the five, you know, the five secrets, I like to say that, you know, if Y.P. says I don’t believe in gravity, I go to the tallest building anywhere in the world and I jump down, and what’s gonna happen to me? I’m gonna go splat. Because whether I believe in it or not, you know, gravity is a universal law, and it always works, right? So the thing about BNI is BNI uses the universal law of success and the universal law of business. And so I see that these five key fundamentals that we always ask members to do are actually fundamental business principles, you know. So what are they?

Number one: all successful people turn up to impress. They just don’t turn up and let things happen. They prepare to make sure that whatever meetings, whatever presentations, they’re always giving their 100%.

Second thing is that, you know, successful leaders are always learning. They’re always learning. You know, the business world is so competitive now we have got to be always upskilling ourselves to do things, you know, better, faster and cheaper than our competitors. Otherwise, we’re gonna get left behind.

And the third thing is building connections. You know, sometimes people think that building connections is all about networking to get clients. But I think it’s more than that. You know, when you talk about building connections, you got to build connections and relationship with authorities, the money people, your bankers, equity investors, you know, so that when you need money to grow your business, they are there. With your suppliers. Okay, we know that in the last couple of years, many businesses had to fold up because their suppliers went bankrupt and so on. So we’ve always got to be networking for plan B, plan C, you know, alternative suppliers.

Then the next one is, you know, the fourth level is your industry, people in your industry, because you’ve got to know where your industry is heading. Sometimes, you know, you got all these big brand names that when they were not, you know, in tune with what the changes in their industry, they got blindsided, and suddenly they become irrelevant, huge telco companies, you know, films. So all these are situations where they got blindsided, they were not in touch, you know, of the changes or the trends in the industry.

And the last one is, of course, networking to get clients. Okay, so these are the five things, which I call the five key fundamentals that we encourage BNI members to do.

Ivan:
Can I just review those real quick for everybody? Turn up to impress; continuous learning, what we call lifelong learning in BNI; build connections and relationships; create value to your business associates; and last, help your clients to grow their business.

Y.P.:
Yeah.

Ivan:
I know one of the things that you recommend on those five points is making sure to do One-to-Ones. You want to talk about that for a moment?

Y.P.:
Okay, sure One-to-Ones is actually, you know, a session where you get to learn more about the other person and find out what they are. It could be at different levels, okay. At initial level, find a bit about their interest, their family, you know, what’s their hot buttons? And later on, as the trust and relationship builds, then you continue on to focus a lot more about their business. You know, what are the target markets? What are the leading questions that you can use to ask them questions?

Ivan:
Yeah. So one of the things you mentioned related to turning up to impress, is to prepare for the meetings. There’s a couple of things that I have in mind. One is, don’t wing it with your weekly presentation.

Y.P.:
True.

Ivan:
Don’t just make it up as you’re speaking, but to come in prepared. What else would you recommend? I’m sure you recommend being prepared for that.

Y.P.:
Yeah. So there are various, you know, activities in BNI, so you mentioned the weekly presentation, there’s a feature presentation, even as you mentioned about One-to-Ones, you know, I always say, come prepared with questions. Okay, so for One-to-Ones always encourage members to exchange their profiles, you know, the speaker profiles, their G.A.I.N.S. profile, so that you can have a read of those questions. And then when we meet, it’s not about finding the details. It’s like asking relevant questions from what you’ve gleaned from reading the profiles, you know? So this is actually helping it to be even more productive as a One-to-One, rather than just basic fact finding.

Ivan:
Yeah, right. And as you do, if you do more One-to-Ones with somebody over the years, you really, you want to go deep. It amazes me how people don’t really know if someone has kids, how old their kids are, how long they’ve been in business. You know, when you know more things about somebody personally and professionally, you build a bond or a relationship with them, wouldn’t you agree?

Y.P.:
Yeah, definitely, definitely. You know, sometimes in a Chapter, you ask the members, do you know the chapter members well? And everybody will raise their hand and say, Yes. Then you ask them, do you know whether they are married? You know your members, are they married, with the kids and so on? And they go, “Ah, uhhh…”

Ivan:
Right. Yeah. And that’s where the One-to-Ones come in helpful and the [profile and G.A.I.N.S] exchange. One of the things that I’ve been saying in recent years that I didn’t say in the beginning is that I recommend, if you’re going to do the G.A.I.N.S. exchange, that you do your goals first, then I do my goals, then you do your accomplishments, then I do my accomplishments. One of the problems I ran into in the early days was somebody, if you have an hour, somebody takes 45 minutes to do going through their list and the other person only has 15. Love One-to-Ones, but share, go back and forth as you do it. Anything else you want to tag on to those five steps?

Y.P.:
Actually, you know, one of the good things about BNI is that now these are the five key things that all successful business owners do, and BNI has got the traffic lights, or we call the members traffic lights. It actually is a success indicator. I don’t see members traffic lights as a report card. I see that it’s a success indicator when I’m doing one of the five key fundamentals. Well, it gives me a green indicator to say that, hey, you’re doing well, Y.P., keep it up. And for those that I’m just started, not doing so well, I could get a red indicator. So actually, we’ve got a Success System, or success tracking system in BNI, to help us focus on the five key fundamentals to make sure that we are successful not only in BNI, but also in our business.

Ivan:
Yep. I mean the things that you’re talking about — turning up and lifelong learning and creating value, these are things that don’t apply just to BNI, as you said. They’re things that apply to any successful business. Here with traffic lights… the traffic lights measure things that will help you be very successful within BNI. And many of those things are, like, turning up, you know, showing up to the meeting. But there are other things, you know, green, you’re doing a great job if you’re in the green. Yellow, you’re doing pretty well, right? Red – you really need to start working on the fundamentals. And gray, Y.P., what do you think about the gray?

Y.P.:
I would say gray is showing that you haven’t even started on that. Okay, that is what area you got to start working on, yeah, and build yourself in that fundamental until you become green.

Ivan:
Yeah. So the things that you do to help move you up to traffic lights to certainly yellow and up to green are really important to help you be successful. I’ve had some people, maybe you’ve had this, too, Y.P., that have said to me, “Well, you know, I can just do things that manipulate it, and I can move myself into the green.” And yeah, that’s true, you can. You can do it for a month, maybe two, but you can’t do it for six months. You gotta actually be doing this stuff.

Y.P.:
Yeah.

Ivan:
We’re out of time. Y.P.. Any last thought before I hand it back over to Priscilla?

Y.P.:
Okay, I just say that for those BNI members listening, this is a success system. BNI is not plug and play, but it’s so easy to plug into the five key fundamentals and just do it and you’ll be successful in your life and your business through BNI.

Ivan:
Said by somebody who has had a lot of success in the organization. Y.P., thank you so much for being on BNI Podcast.

Y.P.:
Thank you.

Ivan:
Over to you, Priscilla.

Priscilla:
Okay, great. Well, I think that’s it for this week. Thank you both so much for the great information.

This podcast is sponsored by www.misneraudioprograms.com. These audio programs will provide you with the tools and the inspiration to powerfully enhance your BNI experience. So check out the great material available to you at www.misneraudioprograms.com and then use the promo code IVAN50 for 50% off of everything [this code is good for a limited time]. All of the proceeds go to the BNI Foundation.

Thank you so much for listening. This is Priscilla Rice, and we look forward to having you join us again next week for another exciting episode of The Official BNI Podcast.
